content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to accept 5 elements in an array.
Then traverse the array and display all elements using a loop.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer NNN, denoting the number of elements in the array.
The second lind of input contains NNN integers separated by a space denoting the elements of the array.


Output Format
Print the elements of the array separated by a space.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 5), denoting the number of elements in the array. The second line contains N integers separated by a space denoting the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: Print the elements of the array separated by a space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: N=5; the program echoes the five numbers in the same order.
N=3; the three numbers are printed unchanged.
Single element array; the sole value is output.

solution.approach: Read the integer N, then read the N array elements. Since no processing is required, simply output the elements in the same order separated by a space. The algorithm runs in O(N) time and uses O(N) auxiliary space (or O(1) if printed directly).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) (or O(1) if printed without storing)

code.Python3:
import sys

data = sys.stdin.read()
# replace the escaped newline sequence with a real space
data = data.replace('\\n', ' ')
tokens = data.split()
if not tokens:
    sys.exit()
N = int(tokens[0])
arr = tokens[1:1+N]
print(' '.join(arr))
